report_name: ICS Patch Tuesday: Siemens, Schneider, Phoenix Contact Release Critical Advisories

tools.name: OpenSSL
tools.description: The openssl.exe command line tool was mentioned as a process to monitor on Windows-based engineering workstations for abnormal usage, which could indicate exploitation attempts against the OpenSSL vulnerability CVE-2025-15467.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2025-15467
vulnerabilities.description: A critical remote code execution flaw in the OpenSSL library affecting a wide range of Siemens products.

vulnerabilities.name: Siemens Sinec INS Vulnerabilities
vulnerabilities.description: Multiple flaws in the Sinec INS industrial network services platform, including authenticated command execution, information disclosure, privilege escalation, and password exposure.

vulnerabilities.name: Siemens Siprotec 5 Vulnerability
vulnerabilities.description: A denial-of-service (DoS) and potential code execution vulnerability in Siprotec 5 protection relays.

vulnerabilities.name: Siemens WinCC Certificate Manager Vulnerability
vulnerabilities.description: A sensitive information exposure weakness in the WinCC Certificate Manager.

vulnerabilities.name: Schneider PowerLogic P7 Vulnerabilities
vulnerabilities.description: Denial-of-service and command execution vulnerabilities in the PowerLogic P7 series.

vulnerabilities.name: Schneider EasyLogic/Saitel Vulnerabilities
vulnerabilities.description: Credential exposure issues in EasyLogic T150 & Saitel DP RTU products.

vulnerabilities.name: Schneider EcoStruxure IT Data Center Expert Vulnerability
vulnerabilities.description: An information disclosure flaw in the EcoStruxure IT Data Center Expert product.
affected_products: Schneider EcoStruxure IT Data Center Expert

vulnerabilities.name: Phoenix Contact CHARX SEC-3xxx Vulnerability
vulnerabilities.description: An unauthenticated log download vulnerability in the firmware of CHARX SEC-3xxx series charging controllers for electric vehicles.

indicators.name: OpenSSL Execution on Engineering Workstation
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: OpenSSL Execution on Engineering Workstation
id: 7f5b1a3c-1e2d-4b8a-9c0d-5e6f7a8b9c0d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of openssl.exe, which could be used in attempts to exploit CVE-2025-15467. Its presence or abnormal usage on an engineering workstation warrants investigation.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/ics-patch-tuesday-vulnerabilities-fixed-by-siemens-schneider-phoenix-contact/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-10
tags:
  - attack.t1059
level: low
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\openssl.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of openssl.exe, which was noted as a hunting hint for potential exploitation of CVE-2025-15467.

indicators.name: Anomalous IT Protocol Traffic in OT Segment
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous IT Protocol Traffic in OT Segment
id: 8c9d0e1f-2a3b-4c5d-6e7f-8a9b0c1d2e3f
status: experimental
description: Detects common IT protocols (SSH, FTP, HTTP/S) directed towards an OT network segment. This could indicate unauthorized access or lateral movement. The destination IP range should be customized to the specific OT environment.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/ics-patch-tuesday-vulnerabilities-fixed-by-siemens-schneider-phoenix-contact/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-10
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative traffic to OT devices.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: network_connection
detection:
  selection:
    DestinationPort:
      - 21
      - 22
      - 80
      - 443
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects unexpected network connections using IT protocols (SSH, FTP, HTTP) to devices that normally only use OT protocols, as suggested in the hunting hints.

indicators.name: Potential Unexplained PLC Mode Change
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Unexplained PLC Mode Change
id: 9a0b1c2d-3e4f-5a6b-7c8d-9e0f1a2b3c4d
status: experimental
description: Detects log entries that may indicate an unexplained PLC or RTU mode change, such as from 'Run' to 'Program' or 'Stop'. Such changes outside of scheduled maintenance could indicate malicious activity.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/ics-patch-tuesday-vulnerabilities-fixed-by-siemens-schneider-phoenix-contact/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-10
tags:
  - attack.t0858
  - attack.t0831
level: high
logsource:
  category: application
detection:
  keywords:
    - 'mode change to program'
    - 'mode change to stop'
    - 'controller state changed to program'
    - 'PLC mode set to PROG'
    - 'logic change'
    - 'firmware update initiated'
  condition: keywords
indicators.description: Detects log entries for unexplained PLC/RTU logic changes, firmware updates, or mode changes (e.g., from 'Run' to 'Program'), as suggested in the hunting hints.

relationships.source_name: Anomalous IT Protocol Traffic in OT Segment
